seoTitle: Arts / Humanities after 10th — courses, exams & careers | SkillKite
seoDescription: After 10th Arts — humanities streams, law, design, government-job tracks

guide.heading: Your options after 10th — SkillKite
guide.greeting: Hi there! You told us you are interested in Arts / Humanities and want to both study and earn. The most relevant options for you are listed first — read through all of them before deciding.

sections.title: Study options
sections.intro: Most relevant option is listed first — but read all options before deciding.

options.name: 12th Arts / Humanities
options.whatIsIt: A 2-year senior secondary stream covering subjects like History, Geography, Political Science, Psychology, Sociology, Fine Arts, and Literature. It is the most flexible and creative stream available after 10th.
options.whoFor: Students who enjoy reading, writing, debating, drawing, performing arts, or understanding society and people.
options.leadsTo: BA, BFA, BJournalism, BDesign, BBA, Law (CLAT), Teaching (BEd), Civil Services (UPSC/State PSC), Mass Communication, Social Work, and many more.
options.keyExams: CUET for central universities, state board Class 12 exams, CLAT for law, NID/NIFT for design

options.name: Polytechnic Diploma (3 years, direct after 10th)
options.whatIsIt: A government-recognised technical diploma offered by polytechnic colleges. Arts students can pursue diplomas in fields like Interior Design, Fashion Design, Printing Technology, or Computer Applications.
options.whoFor: Students who want a practical, skill-based qualification and prefer learning by doing over sitting in regular classrooms.
options.leadsTo: Jobs in design studios, printing industry, IT support, or lateral entry into the 2nd year of a relevant degree (B.Tech / B.Voc) in many states.
options.keyExams: State Polytechnic Entrance Exam (varies by state — e.g., JEECUP in UP, DET in Maharashtra). Admission criteria differ, so check your state board.

options.name: 12th Commerce
options.whatIsIt: A 2-year stream covering Accountancy, Business Studies, Economics, and optionally Mathematics. It is a strong foundation for business, finance, and entrepreneurship careers.
options.whoFor: Arts students who also have an interest in business, money management, or starting their own venture.
options.leadsTo: BCom, BBA, CA (Chartered Accountancy), Company Secretary, Banking, Finance, and E-commerce roles.
options.keyExams: CUET for central universities, state board Class 12 exams, CA Foundation after 12th

options.name: 12th Science with Maths (PCM)
options.whatIsIt: A challenging 2-year stream with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics. It is among the most demanding streams and requires strong interest and commitment in these subjects.
options.whoFor: Students with genuine interest in Mathematics and logical problem-solving. Not typically the first choice for Arts-oriented students, but possible if you want to switch direction.
options.leadsTo: Engineering (JEE), Architecture (NATA), BCA, BSc, Defence, Merchant Navy, and more.
options.keyExams: JEE Main and JEE Advanced for engineering, NATA for architecture, CUET for BSc

options.name: 12th Science with Biology (PCB)
options.whatIsIt: A 2-year stream with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology. NEET is extremely competitive — only a small percentage of lakhs of applicants secure MBBS seats each year.
options.whoFor: Students with a strong interest in medicine or life sciences. Not typically suited for Arts-inclined students unless you have a specific medical goal.
options.leadsTo: MBBS, BDS, BAMS, Nursing, Physiotherapy, Biotechnology, and allied health sciences.
options.keyExams: NEET-UG (very competitive — around 2 million students appear annually for limited seats)

options.name: 12th Science with Maths + Biology (PCMB)
options.whatIsIt: A 2-year stream that combines all four core science subjects — Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, and Biology — keeping both engineering and medical options open simultaneously.
options.whoFor: Students who are truly undecided between engineering and medicine and are willing to handle a heavier study load. Rarely the right fit for Arts students.
options.leadsTo: Both engineering (JEE) and medical (NEET) pathways, plus BSc, BPharm, and other science degrees.
options.keyExams: JEE Main / JEE Advanced for engineering, NEET-UG for medical

options.name: Paramedical Diploma after 10th (DMLT, ANM, X-Ray Tech, etc.)
options.whatIsIt: Short vocational diplomas in health-related technical roles such as Lab Technician (DMLT), Auxiliary Nurse Midwife (ANM), X-Ray Technician, and similar. These are typically more suited to Science/Biology students.
options.whoFor: Students who want to enter the healthcare sector quickly without pursuing a full science degree. Possible for any stream student in some states — check local eligibility rules.
options.leadsTo: Entry-level jobs in hospitals, clinics, diagnostic labs, and government health centres.
options.keyExams: State-level paramedical entrance tests or direct admission based on 10th marks (varies by state and institute)
options.timeCommitment: 1 to 2 years depending on the diploma

sections.title: Job & earning options
sections.intro: Since you want to both study and earn, these are realistic ways to build income skills alongside your studies. None of these require you to quit school — many students run these part-time.

options.name: Content Creation (YouTube / Instagram / Blogging)
options.whatIsIt: Creating videos, reels, or written posts around topics you love — art, travel, storytelling, education, or anything you are passionate about. Arts students often have a natural advantage here.
options.whoFor: Students who are creative, comfortable on camera or behind a keyboard, and enjoy communicating ideas.
options.leadsTo: Ad revenue, brand collaborations, freelance writing, social media management, and a long-term personal brand.
options.timeCommitment: Can start immediately; results typically show in 6 to 18 months of consistent effort

options.name: Graphic Design (Canva / Figma)
options.whatIsIt: Learning to create posters, social media graphics, logos, and presentations using free or low-cost tools like Canva and Figma. Arts students often pick this up quickly.
options.whoFor: Students with an eye for visuals, colour, and layout who enjoy making things look good.
options.leadsTo: Freelance design work on platforms like Fiverr and Internshala, part-time jobs with small businesses, and eventually UI/UX or professional graphic design careers.
options.timeCommitment: 2 to 3 months to learn basics; freelancing can begin within 3 to 4 months

options.name: Photography and Videography
options.whatIsIt: Using a smartphone or basic camera to offer photography or video editing services for events, social media, or local businesses. Arts students often have a strong visual sense that helps here.
options.whoFor: Students who enjoy observing the world creatively, have a decent smartphone, and are willing to practise consistently.
options.leadsTo: Event photography, wedding shoots, YouTube editing, short film making, and eventually a professional media career.
options.timeCommitment: Can start practising immediately; earning potential builds over 3 to 6 months

options.name: Meesho / Reselling
options.whatIsIt: Selling products online through platforms like Meesho or GlowRoad without holding any inventory. You earn a margin on every sale you make through your social network or online listings.
options.whoFor: Students who are comfortable using a smartphone and have a social network they can reach out to.
options.leadsTo: Pocket money to begin with, and over time the skills of online selling, customer handling, and digital marketing that are very valuable in business.
options.timeCommitment: Can start within a week; earnings depend on effort and network size

options.name: Tailoring and Stitching
options.whatIsIt: Learning to stitch, alter, or design clothes through a local course or online tutorials. This is a practical skill with steady local demand.
options.whoFor: Students interested in fashion, design, or craft who want a tangible, hands-on skill.
options.leadsTo: Home-based tailoring income, boutique work, fashion design courses, and eventually your own label or workshop.
options.timeCommitment: Basic course: 3 to 6 months; earning can begin soon after

options.name: Data Entry and Typing
options.whatIsIt: Offering data entry, form-filling, or typing services to small businesses and professionals. It is straightforward work that pays consistently once you build speed and accuracy.
options.whoFor: Students with decent typing speed and attention to detail who want reliable, low-barrier earning.
options.leadsTo: Freelance gigs on platforms like Fiverr or local businesses, and it builds computer skills useful in any office career.
options.timeCommitment: Can start within 1 to 2 months of basic computer and typing practice

options.name: Mobile Phone Repair
options.whatIsIt: Learning to diagnose and fix common smartphone problems such as screen replacement, charging port issues, and software resets. Short courses are available in most towns.
options.whoFor: Students who are curious about how devices work and enjoy hands-on technical problem-solving.
options.leadsTo: Your own repair shop, part-time income while studying, and a foundation for electronics or IT careers.
options.timeCommitment: Short course: 1 to 3 months; earning can begin soon after

options.name: Tally and Basic Accounting
options.whatIsIt: Learning Tally software and basic bookkeeping to help small shops and businesses maintain their accounts. This is more relevant if you are also considering Commerce — but any student can learn it.
options.whoFor: Students who are comfortable with numbers and want a skill that is in constant demand among small businesses across India.
options.leadsTo: Part-time bookkeeping jobs, accounting assistant roles, and a strong foundation if you later pursue Commerce or CA.

guide.closingMessage: Save this guide and discuss it with your parents or teachers. When you finish 12th, come back to SkillKite — we'll build you a detailed career roadmap. Share it with a friend who needs it too. 🪁
